Savings.High-Yield Savings Account: Offered by banks/credit unions with APY 4-5% (e.g., Marcus, Ally)
Savings.Money Market Account: Similar to savings but often with check-writing; FDIC insured
Savings.Treasury Bills: Short-term US govt securities (4-week to 52-week); near-risk-free
Savings.CD (Certificate of Deposit): Fixed-term deposits with guaranteed return; FDIC insured

Retirement.401(k): Employer-sponsored plan; pre-tax or Roth contributions; employer match common
Retirement.IRA (Traditional/Roth): Individual Retirement Account; $7,000/year limit (2024); tax advantages
Retirement.SEP-IRA: For self-employed/small business owners; higher contribution limits
Retirement.Social Security: Government retirement benefit; starts at 62 (reduced) or 67 (full)

Investment.Brokerage Account: Taxable account for stocks, ETFs, mutual funds (e.g., Fidelity, Schwab)
Investment.ETFs: Low-cost diversified funds; e.g., SPY (S&P 500), QQQ (Nasdaq-100)
Investment.REITs: Real Estate Investment Trusts; traded like stocks; dividend income
Investment.I-Bonds: Inflation-linked US savings bonds; up to $10,000/year

Buying house.FHA Loan: 3.5% down payment; lower credit score requirements
Buying house.VA Loan: 0% down for eligible veterans/active military; no PMI
Buying house.USDA Loan: 0% down for rural properties; income limits apply

Savings.TFSA (Tax-Free Savings Account): Contributions not tax-deductible, but growth and withdrawals tax-free
Savings.HISA (High-Interest Savings Account): Offered by EQ Bank, Tangerine; current rates ~4-5%

Retirement.RRSP (Registered Retirement Savings Plan: Pre-tax contributions; 18% of previous year income limit
Retirement.CPP (Canada Pension Plan): Mandatory payroll deduction; benefits from age 60-70
Retirement.OAS (Old Age Security): Universal pension at 65; income-tested supplement (GIS) available

Investment.TFSA Investing: Invest in stocks/ETFs/bonds inside TFSA; all growth tax-free
Investment.Dividend Stocks: Canadian banks/utilities known for reliable dividends
Investment.REITs: Canadian REITs (e.g., RioCan, Canadian Apartment REIT)

Buying house.HBP (Home Buyers' Plan): Withdraw up to $35,000 from RRSP tax-free for first home
Buying house.CMHC Mortgage Insurance: Required if down payment < 20%; enables low down payment mortgages
Buying house.First-Time Home Buyer Incentive: Gov't shared equity; 5-10% of purchase price

Savings.NSC (National Savings Certificate): 5-year post office scheme; ~7.7% p.a.; tax deductible u/s 80C
Savings.Fixed Deposits: Bank FDs; currently ~7-8% p.a.; TDS applicable
Savings.Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana: For girl child; ~8.2% p.a.; 80C deduction; 21-year maturity

Retirement.PMVVY (PM Vaya Vandana Yojana): LIC pension plan for seniors; guaranteed 7.4% p.a. for 10 years

Investment.Mutual Funds (SIP): Systematic Investment Plans; ELSS for tax-saving; diversified equity/debt
Investment.Direct Equity: NSE/BSE stocks; Demat account via Zerodha, Groww, etc.
Investment.REITs: Embassy REIT, Mindspace REIT; yields ~6-8%; listed on NSE
Investment.Sovereign Gold Bonds: RBI-issued; 2.5% interest + gold price appreciation; tax-free on maturity

Buying house.PMAY (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana): Subsidised home loans for EWS/LIG/MIG; interest subsidy up to 6.5%
Buying house.NHB (National Housing Bank) Schemes: Refinance schemes for affordable housing loans
Buying house.State Housing Board Schemes: DDA, MHADA, etc.; lottery-based affordable housing

Savings.Cash ISA: Savings account within ISA wrapper; rates ~4-5% currently
Savings.Premium Bonds: NS&I lottery bonds; tax-free prizes; 1-month prize draw
Savings.Fixed-Rate Savings Bonds: Offered by NS&I, banks; competitive rates for fixed terms

Retirement.State Pension: Full new state pension ~£221/week (2024); 35 qualifying NI years needed
Retirement.SIPP (Self-Invested Personal Pension): Flexible pension; invest in wide range of assets; tax relief on contributions
Retirement.Lifetime ISA: For under-40s; 25% government bonus; use for first home or retirement

Investment.Stocks & Shares ISA: Invest in equities/funds within ISA; all gains tax-free
Investment.Investment Trusts: Listed funds; e.g., Scottish Mortgage, City of London
Investment.VCT/EIS: Venture Capital Trusts/Enterprise Investment Scheme; high-risk, high tax relief

Buying house.Help to Buy ISA: Closed to new applicants; existing accounts usable until 2030
Buying house.Lifetime ISA: Save up to £4,000/year; 25% gov bonus; use for first home (up to £450k)
Buying house.Shared Ownership: Buy 25-75% of property; pay rent on rest; ladder up over time
Buying house.First Homes Scheme: 30-50% discount for first-time buyers on new-build homes

Savings.KiwiSaver: Voluntary (mandatory after joining workforce); 3-10% of pay; employer match
Savings.Term Deposits: Bank fixed-term deposits; rates ~5-6% p.a. currently
Savings.Bonus Bonds: Discontinued but bank savings accounts offer competitive rates
Savings.Simplicity / InvestNow: Low-fee NZ investment platforms for savings/index funds

Retirement.KiwiSaver Retirement: Access at 65; employer + employee contributions; government member tax credits
Retirement.NZ Superannuation: Universal pension at 65; ~$24,000/year for couples; no means test
Retirement.Defined Benefit Schemes: Government employee schemes (e.g., NZDF, teachers); employer-funded
Retirement.Self-Managed Retirement: Index funds, rental property, or term deposits outside KiwiSaver

Investment.Sharesies / Hatch: Retail investment platforms for NZ and global stocks/ETFs
Investment.Rental Property: Common NZ investment; note Bright-line test (10 years for most)
Investment.PIE Funds: Portfolio Investment Entity tax structure; capped at 28% PIR tax

Buying house.First Home Grant: Up to $10,000 for existing homes, $20,000 for new builds (income/price limits)
Buying house.First Home Loan: 5% deposit with Kāinga Ora guarantee; via select banks
Buying house.KiwiSaver HomeStart: Withdraw KiwiSaver contributions (not returns) for first home
Buying house.Shared Equity Schemes: Kāinga Ora First Home Partner; government co-ownership

Savings.Spaarrekening (Savings Account): Standard savings account; rates recovering post-ECB hikes (~2-3%)
Savings.Box 3 Savings: Tax on deemed return of savings/investments; rate ~6.17% (2024)
Savings.Deposito: Fixed-term deposit; major banks offer 1-3 year terms
Savings.ASN / Triodos Green Savings: Sustainable savings accounts with competitive rates

Retirement.AOW (Algemene Ouderdomswet): State pension; full pension at 67; built up at 2%/year
Retirement.Pensioenregeling (Workplace Pension): Mandatory sector-wide or company pension funds (e.g., ABP, PFZW)
Retirement.Lijfrente (Annuity): Tax-deferred annuity product; deduct contributions from income
Retirement.BankSparen: Banking-based pension savings; lower cost alternative to insurance annuities

Investment.Beleggingsrekening: Investment account; stocks/ETFs/bonds via DEGIRO, ABN AMRO
Investment.DEGIRO: Low-cost Dutch online broker; access to global ETFs and stocks
Investment.Vastgoed (Real Estate): Buy-to-let market; note Box 3 deemed return tax
Investment.VBI (Vrijgestelde Beleggingsinstelling): Tax-exempt investment vehicle for larger portfolios

Buying house.NHG (Nationale Hypotheek Garantie): Government mortgage guarantee; lower rates; limit ~€435,000
Buying house.Eigen Woning Rente Aftrek: Mortgage interest deduction; being phased down to 37.05%
Buying house.Starterslening: Municipality top-up loan for first-time buyers; interest-free initially
Buying house.Koopsubsidie: Subsidy for lower-income first-time buyers via municipalities

Savings.Tagesgeld (Daily Money Account): Instant-access savings; rates ~3-4% at direct banks (ING, DKB)
Savings.Festgeld (Fixed-Term Deposit): Locked savings; higher rates for 1-5 year terms
Savings.Bausparvertrag: Building savings contract for future property purchase; state bonus
Savings.Bundesschatzbriefe: Federal savings bonds; government-backed; various maturities

Retirement.Gesetzliche Rentenversicherung: Mandatory state pension; 18.6% payroll contribution (split employee/employer)
Retirement.Riester-Rente: State-subsidised private pension; €175/year bonus + child bonuses
Retirement.R\u00fcrup-Rente (Basisrente): For self-employed; deductible contributions; annuity at retirement
Retirement.Betriebliche Altersvorsorge (bAV): Company pension; employer contributions + tax/social security benefits

Investment.ETF-Sparplan: Monthly ETF savings plan; popular via Scalable Capital, Trade Republic
Investment.Depot (Brokerage Account): Via Comdirect, ING; invest in stocks, bonds, ETFs
Investment.Immobilienfonds: Real estate funds (open-ended); indirect property investment
Investment.Crowdinvesting: Real estate crowdfunding via platforms like Exporo, Bergfürst

Buying house.KfW Wohneigentumsprogramm: Low-interest KfW loans for home purchase; up to €100,000
Buying house.Wohn-Riester: Riester pension used for owner-occupied property financing
Buying house.Bausparvertrag: Savings + low-interest loan contract for home purchase
Buying house.Regional F\u00f6rderbanken: State development bank loans (e.g., L-Bank, NRW.BANK)

Savings.Livret A: Tax-free savings account; rate 3% (as of 2024); €22,950 ceiling

Retirement.Assurance Retraite (CNAV): State pension; full rate at 67 or 43 years of contributions
Retirement.PER (Plan d'\u00c9pargne Retraite): New unified private pension; contributions tax-deductible
Retirement.PERCO/PEROB: Company collective retirement savings plan; employer matching
Retirement.AGIRC-ARRCO: Mandatory supplementary pension for private sector employees

Investment.PEA (Plan d'\u00c9pargne en Actions): €150,000 limit; invest in EU stocks; tax-free after 5 years
Investment.Assurance-vie (UC): Unit-linked insurance; invest in funds; tax advantages after 8 years
Investment.SCPI (Real Estate Investment): Sociétés Civiles de Placement Immobilier; indirect real estate
Investment.Crowdfunding Immobilier: Real estate crowdfunding; platforms like Homunity, Anaxago

Buying house.PTZ (Pr\u00eat \u00e0 Taux Z\u00e9ro): Zero-interest loan for first-time buyers; income/location conditions
Buying house.PAS (Pr\u00eat d'Accession Sociale): Low-rate social home loan; income limits; APL mortgage aid
Buying house.Action Logement (1% Logement): Employer housing loan; subsidised rate for employees
Buying house.Dispositif Pinel: Tax reduction for buying new-build rental property; being phased out

Savings.High-Interest Savings Account: Online banks (ING, UBank, ME Bank); bonus rates ~5% with conditions
Savings.Term Deposits: Fixed-term bank deposits; ~4.5-5.5% p.a. for 1-2 year terms
Savings.Offset Account: Mortgage offset; reduces interest on home loan; effective savings rate = mortgage rate
Savings.Cash Management Account: Higher returns than transaction accounts; instant access

Retirement.Superannuation: Mandatory 11% (rising to 12%) employer contribution; concessional tax rate
Retirement.SMSF (Self-Managed Super Fund): Control your own super; invest in property, shares, etc.
Retirement.Transition to Retirement (TTR): Access super as income stream from 60 while still working

Investment.Managed Funds: Vanguard, Fidelity; actively managed or index; via financial advisor
Investment.Investment Bonds: Tax-paid bonds; 30% tax on earnings; tax-free withdrawals after 10 years
Investment.Property (Negative Gearing): Common strategy; interest/costs deduct against income; CGT discount

Buying house.First Home Super Saver Scheme: Voluntary super contributions; withdraw up to $50,000 for first home
Buying house.Stamp Duty Concessions: State-specific; exemptions/discounts for first-time buyers and lower-value homes
